vocabulary.meaning: to perform an exam, test, or course
vocabulary.example: I have to take a biology exam tomorrow morning.

vocabulary.meaning: to be successful in an exam, test, or course
vocabulary.example: She was delighted to pass her driving test.

vocabulary.meaning: to look at and understand the meaning of written or printed words
vocabulary.example: I like to read the newspaper over breakfast.

vocabulary.meaning: to learn about a subject, especially in an educational course or by reading books
vocabulary.example: He wants to study medicine at university.

vocabulary.meaning: a short exam to see how much you know or how well you can do something
vocabulary.example: We have a quick spelling test every Friday.

vocabulary.meaning: an official test of knowledge or ability in a particular subject
vocabulary.example: He is studying hard for his final school exams.

vocabulary.meaning: relating to the education of children between the ages of about five and eleven
vocabulary.example: In the UK, primary school begins at age four or five.

vocabulary.meaning: relating to the education of children between the ages of eleven and sixteen or eighteen
vocabulary.example: After primary school, students move on to secondary school.

vocabulary.meaning: a school for students between the ages of eleven and eighteen
vocabulary.example: She goes to a high school in Chicago.

vocabulary.meaning: a person that you work with
vocabulary.example: He discussed the proposal with his colleagues.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who is in the same class as you at school or college
vocabulary.example: She invited all her classmates to her birthday party.

vocabulary.meaning: an older student in a school who is given some authority and helps to control the younger students
vocabulary.example: He was chosen to be a school prefect.

vocabulary.meaning: a child at school who is being taught
vocabulary.example: The school has over five hundred pupils.

vocabulary.meaning: a person who is studying at a university, college, or school
vocabulary.example: He is a student at the local college.

vocabulary.meaning: an official record showing that you have finished a training course or have the necessary skills
vocabulary.example: You will need professional qualifications for this job.

vocabulary.meaning: a characteristic or feature of someone or something
vocabulary.example: Honesty is one of her best qualities.

vocabulary.meaning: to say numbers one after another in order, or calculate the total number
vocabulary.example: She can count from one to ten in French.

vocabulary.meaning: to find the size, quantity, or degree of something
vocabulary.example: He measured the width of the table.

vocabulary.meaning: a course of study at a college or university, or the qualification given to a student who completes this
vocabulary.example: She has a physics degree from Oxford.

vocabulary.meaning: an official document that states that information on it is true or that you have completed a course
vocabulary.example: He received a certificate for completing the first aid course.

vocabulary.meaning: the mark or grade that you get in an exam or test
vocabulary.example: The exam results will be posted next week.

vocabulary.meaning: to use your voice to say words in a language
vocabulary.example: Can you speak Spanish?

vocabulary.meaning: to say things to someone as part of a conversation
vocabulary.example: They talked about their school days for hours.

vocabulary.meaning: a period of time in which students are taught a subject or activity
vocabulary.example: We have an English lesson on Monday mornings.

vocabulary.meaning: an area of knowledge that you study in a school or university
vocabulary.example: Math is my favorite subject.

vocabulary.meaning: to succeed in doing or getting something as a result of your actions
vocabulary.example: She worked hard to achieve her goals.

vocabulary.meaning: to arrive at a particular point, stage, or level
vocabulary.example: The temperature reached thirty degrees yesterday.

vocabulary.meaning: a piece of work that has to be done, especially one that is difficult or unpleasant
vocabulary.example: He was given the task of cleaning the classroom.

vocabulary.meaning: physical or mental activity needed to achieve something
vocabulary.example: Writing a book takes a lot of time and effort.

vocabulary.meaning: to have information in your mind
vocabulary.example: I know the answer to this question.

vocabulary.meaning: to know someone or something because you have seen, heard, or experienced them before
vocabulary.example: I didn't recognize him with his new haircut.

vocabulary.meaning: to show someone how to do something or give lessons in a school
vocabulary.example: She teaches biology at the high school.

vocabulary.meaning: to gain knowledge or skill in a new subject or activity
vocabulary.example: He is learning to play the piano.

vocabulary.meaning: a selective secondary school in the UK for academic students
vocabulary.example: He passed the entrance exam to get into the grammar school.

vocabulary.meaning: a secondary school in the UK for children of all abilities
vocabulary.example: Most children in the area attend the local comprehensive school.

vocabulary.meaning: the scientific study of stars, planets, and space
vocabulary.example: She has been interested in astronomy since she was a child.

vocabulary.meaning: Master of Arts, a postgraduate degree in an arts or social science subject
vocabulary.example: He is studying for an MA in history.

vocabulary.meaning: required by law or a rule; obligatory
vocabulary.example: Wearing a school uniform is compulsory.

vocabulary.meaning: to talk with excessive pride and self-satisfaction about one's achievements
vocabulary.example: He boasted about his high marks to everyone.

vocabulary.meaning: conforming with generally accepted standards of respectable behavior, or of a satisfactory quality
vocabulary.example: He got a decent job after graduating.

phrasalVerbs.example: EX: It took me a while to catch on to what they were doing.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: be persuaded to change your mind or opinion
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I'm sure he'll come round to our way of thinking in the end.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: draw a line through something written
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: If you make a mistake, just cross it out.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: suddenly realize something for the first time
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: It suddenly dawned on me that I had left my keys inside.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: handle, cope with, or take action about
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: We need to deal with this problem before it gets worse.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: leave school, college, etc. without finishing your studies
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: He dropped out of university after the first term.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: try to express or suggest something
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I don't understand what you are getting at.
EX: Stop talking and get on with your work.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: stop competing or arguing and accept you cannot win
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: I resisted for a long time but finally gave in.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: stop doing something you do regularly
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: He decided to give up smoking.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: move or make progress at the same rate as someone or something else
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: It's hard to keep up with the latest technological developments.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: reach the same point or level as someone else
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Run ahead and I will catch up with you later.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: do or deal with something very easily
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: She sailed through her exams without any difficulty.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: explain, describe, or arrange something in a clear, detailed way
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: He set out his ideas in a clear presentation.

phrasalVerbs.meaning: consider a problem or decision carefully
phrasalVerbs.example: EX: Let me think it over before I give you an answer.

collocations.meaning: perform school work at home
possess school work that needs to be completed at home
listen to, watch, or consider something or someone carefully
make someone notice you or look at you
make someone notice a particular fact or situation

collocations.collocation: take / have a break from sth/doing
collocations.meaning: stop doing an activity for a short period of time to rest
a period of time in the middle of the day for eating lunch
a short period of time when you stop working to have a drink
a short interruption in a television programme to show advertisements
allow someone to rest, or stop criticizing them

collocations.collocation: have a discussion (with sb) about/on sth/doing
collocations.meaning: talk about a topic in detail with someone
sit for an official test of knowledge
succeed in an official test of knowledge
be unsuccessful in an official test of knowledge
take an exam as a student
express doubt about an opinion or suggestion
possess a thought, plan, or suggestion
a very clever or useful thought or suggestion
not know anything about something

collocations.collocation: have a lot to learn about sth/doing
collocations.meaning: need to acquire much more knowledge or skill about something
acquire the skill or knowledge needed to perform an action
attend a class to be taught a subject
participate in a class session
a school session that lasts for two class periods consecutively
understand what not to do in future as a result of a bad experience
show someone that their bad behavior has unpleasant consequences
succeed in understanding something complicated or unclear
it is reasonable or sensible to perform an action
the ability to appreciate humour, taste, or sight

collocations.collocation: make up your mind about sth/doing
collocations.meaning: make a final decision about something
remember and consider something

collocations.collocation: in two minds abt sth / doing
collocations.meaning: unable to decide between two choices
change your opinion or decision about something
come into your thoughts briefly or unexpectedly
in my opinion
not object if something happens
be crazy, or be extremely worried about something
according to my own thoughts or beliefs
state what you think about a topic
possess a particular belief or attitude about something
hand something to another person
succeed in a test or exam
go past a structure
understand someone's argument or opinion
understand the purpose or usefulness of something
it is not worth doing because it won't achieve anything
always do something or take particular care to do it
offer or agree to a plan or idea

wordPatterns.meaning: having the power, skill, or opportunity to do something
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is able to speak three languages fluently.

wordPatterns.meaning: respect and approve of someone for their actions
wordPatterns.example: EX: I admire her for her dedication to teaching.

wordPatterns.meaning: talk proudly and proudly about achievements to someone
wordPatterns.example: EX: He boasted about winning the science prize to all his friends.

wordPatterns.meaning: having the ability or qualities necessary for doing something
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is capable of handling the work under pressure.

wordPatterns.meaning: express praise or good wishes to someone for their success
wordPatterns.example: EX: They congratulated him on passing his exams.

wordPatterns.meaning: be unsuccessful in trying to do something
wordPatterns.example: EX: He failed to complete the task within the time limit.

wordPatterns.meaning: want something to happen and plan for it
wordPatterns.example: EX: I hope to study astronomy at college.

wordPatterns.meaning: wish or expect that a situation is true
wordPatterns.example: EX: I hope that you can join us for the trip.

wordPatterns.meaning: acquire knowledge or understanding of a topic
wordPatterns.example: EX: We are learning about ancient history this term.

wordPatterns.meaning: acquire the skill or ability to perform an action
wordPatterns.example: EX: Children learn to read at primary school.

wordPatterns.meaning: acquire skills through practice rather than theory
wordPatterns.example: EX: The best way to learn computer programming is by doing.

wordPatterns.meaning: accept or decide on something, especially if not ideal
wordPatterns.example: EX: They finally settled on a date for the exam.

wordPatterns.meaning: having a resemblance in appearance, character, or quantity
wordPatterns.example: EX: My school uniform is similar to yours.

wordPatterns.meaning: devote time to learning about a subject or preparing for an exam
wordPatterns.example: EX: She is studying hard for her final exams.

wordPatterns.meaning: achieve the desired aim or result in an activity
wordPatterns.example: EX: He succeeded in passing the entrance test.

wordPatterns.meaning: appropriate or right for a particular purpose or situation
wordPatterns.example: EX: This course is suitable for beginners.

wordPatterns.meaning: appropriate to perform a certain action
wordPatterns.example: EX: It is not suitable to wear casual clothes to school.

wordFormation.meaning: academy: a school or college for special training / academic: relating to schools, colleges, and study (adj) or a teacher at a college (n)
wordFormation.example:
EX: She trained at the royal music academy.
EX: He had a very successful academic career.

wordFormation.meaning: attend: be present at an event or school / attention: listening or looking carefully / attendant: someone whose job is to help customers (n) or present/accompanying (adj) / attendance: the number of people present / attentive: listening or watching carefully / inattentive: not paying attention
wordFormation.example:
EX: He attends the local secondary school.
EX: Pay close attention to what the teacher says.
EX: The flight attendant served us drinks.
EX: School attendance has improved this term.
EX: The students were very attentive during the lesson.
EX: He was reprimanded for being inattentive in class.

wordFormation.meaning: behave: act in a particular way / behaviour: the way that someone behaves
wordFormation.example:
EX: The pupils behaved very well during the visit.
EX: His aggressive behaviour shocked everyone.

wordFormation.meaning: certify: state officially that something is true / certificate: an official document stating a fact / certified: having met the official requirements
wordFormation.example:
EX: The doctor certified that he was fit to work.
EX: She received a certificate for public speaking.
EX: He is a certified accountant.

wordFormation.meaning: educate: teach or train someone / education: the process of teaching and learning / educator: a person who teaches / educational: relating to education or providing information
wordFormation.example:
EX: He was educated at a public school.
EX: She believes that education is the key to success.
EX: He is a well-known educator and author.
EX: They went on an educational trip to the museum.

wordFormation.meaning: fail: not succeed in doing something or an exam / failure: lack of success / failing: a weakness in someone's character (n) or in the absence of (prep)
wordFormation.example:
EX: He failed his history test.
EX: The project ended in complete failure.
EX: Pride is his greatest failing.
EX: Failing payment, the service will be suspended.

wordFormation.meaning: improve: make or become better / improvement: an occasion when something gets better / improved: having become better
wordFormation.example:
EX: Her English has improved a lot this year.
EX: We need to see an improvement in your work.
EX: The company released an improved version of the software.

wordFormation.meaning: intense: extreme or very strong / intensity: the quality of being intense / intensify: become or make more intense / intensely: in a very strong or extreme way
wordFormation.example:
EX: The heat from the fire was intense.
EX: The intensity of the light hurt my eyes.
EX: The police intensified their search for the missing child.
EX: She disliked him intensely.

wordFormation.meaning: literate: able to read and write / literacy: the ability to read and write / literature: written works such as novels or plays / illiterate: unable to read and write
wordFormation.example:
EX: Most children are fully literate by the age of seven.
EX: The government is trying to improve adult literacy rates.
EX: She is studying English literature at university.
EX: A large percentage of the population was illiterate.

wordFormation.meaning: reason: a cause or explanation / reasonable: sensible and fair / unreasonable: not fair or sensible / reasoning: the process of thinking about something in a logical way
wordFormation.example:
EX: Give me one good reason why you did this.
EX: It was a perfectly reasonable request.
EX: Her demands were completely unreasonable.
EX: I don't understand the reasoning behind this decision.

wordFormation.meaning: revise: study again to prepare for an exam / revision: the process of revising study material / revised: changed or corrected
wordFormation.example:
EX: You need to revise for your spelling test.
EX: She has done a lot of revision this week.
EX: The publisher released a revised edition of the textbook.

wordFormation.meaning: solve: find an answer to a problem / solution: a way of solving a problem / unsolvable: impossible to solve
wordFormation.example:
EX: He managed to solve the math puzzle.
EX: We need to find a solution to this problem.
EX: Some mathematical problems are unsolvable.

wordFormation.meaning: scholar: a person who studies a subject in detail / scholarship: an agreement to pay for someone's studies / scholastic: relating to schools or education / scholarly: containing serious detailed study
wordFormation.example:
EX: He is a respected scholar of ancient history.
EX: She won a scholarship to study at Oxford.
EX: The school has high scholastic standards.
EX: He wrote a scholarly article on the subject.

wordFormation.meaning: study: learn about a subject / student: a person studying / studies: research or work done to learn (n) / studious: spending a lot of time studying
wordFormation.example:
EX: He is studying for his exams.
EX: The students gathered in the classroom.
EX: She continued her studies at university.
EX: He is a very studious young man.

wordFormation.meaning: teach: show someone how to do something or give lessons / teacher: a person who teaches
wordFormation.example:
EX: She teaches chemistry at the secondary school.
EX: Our teacher explained the new topic clearly.

wordFormation.meaning: think: use your brain to consider something / unthinkable: impossible to imagine or accept / thoughtful: showing consideration or quiet reflection / thoughtfully: in a considerate or reflective way / thoughtless: not thinking about others
wordFormation.example:
EX: Let me think about this for a minute.
EX: A war between the two countries is unthinkable.
EX: It was very thoughtful of you to send flowers.
EX: She stared thoughtfully out of the window.
EX: It was a very thoughtless remark.

wordFormation.meaning: understand: know the meaning of something / misunderstand: fail to understand correctly / misunderstanding: a failure to understand correctly / misunderstood: past form of misunderstand (v) or not appreciated (adj) / understandable: easy to understand or accept
wordFormation.example:
EX: Do you understand the grammar rule?
EX: I'm sorry, I must have misunderstood you.
EX: The argument was due to a simple misunderstanding.
EX: He felt misunderstood by his family.
EX: Her anger was completely understandable under the circumstances.
